summary: Iteration 107 - Phase 1 clinical-correlation bridge fields backend tests. Added pytest suite covering PUT /api/procedures/{id} accepting the 4 new top-level bridge_* fields (bridge_design, bridge_material, bridge_pontics, bridge_implants), persistence via GET, max_length validation (200/80) at and over the boundary, list-type enforcement on the two List[str] fields, partial-update non-clobbering, RBAC denial for non-owner students, and Case Report PDF emission of the 4 labels + values when set plus regression (no 500) when absent. Result: 12/12 new tests pass. Regression run on iteration_103/104/105/106 suites: 73 passed, 2 skipped (nurse-account skips, unchanged from iter_106).
critical_code_review_comments: ProcedureUpdate.bridge_pontics / bridge_implants are typed Optional[List[str]] with no per-element max_length or item-count cap. A malicious client could send thousands of long strings. Consider Field(max_length=8) at the list level and a per-item validator (e.g., regex for FDI tooth numbers 11-48) to prevent garbage from reaching the PDF.

server.py L1719 builds update_data by stripping None values: `{k: v for k, v in procedure_update.model_dump().items() if v is not None}`. This means a client can never CLEAR a bridge_* field by sending null - they can only overwrite it with a new value or empty list. If clinicians need to retract a bridge suggestion, expose either an explicit DELETE flag or treat empty-list / empty-string as an erase signal.

case-report PDF (L4686-4697) only renders bridge_material / bridge_implants / bridge_pontics if bridge_design is also truthy. If a partial save persists material without design (e.g., due to a buggy client flow), those values will silently disappear from the PDF. Recommend rendering each non-empty bridge_* field independently or validating cross-field presence at write time.

ProcedureUpdate has no validator stripping/normalising bridge_design and bridge_material - unlike patient_name etc. which go through sanitize_input. A user-supplied bridge_design with newlines or HTML entities will land verbatim in the PDF (FPDF's safe() does latin-1 replace but does not strip control chars). Add a @field_validator to sanitize_input these two text fields.

test_phase1_bridge_fields_iteration107 mutates a real student-owned draft procedure (id 69d3374795c2d7fbae4b623e) without teardown. Rerunning leaves bridge_* set to test values. Acceptable for a draft, but a class-scope teardown that PUTs the 4 fields back to their pre-test snapshot would be cleaner.
test_credentials: Used Gaurav.pandey / Student@123 (student owner) and Abhijit.patil / Admin@123 (admin) from /app/memory/test_credentials.md. RBAC test located another-student-owned procedure 699fc5c1248100e8a0d87261 via admin list and confirmed 403.
seed_data_creation: No new procedures or users created. Reused draft 69d3374795c2d7fbae4b623e (referenced in the review_request) plus one additional student-owned draft for the absent-fields PDF regression. Bridge_* fields on draft 69d3374795c2d7fbae4b623e were left set to bridge_design='Three-unit implant-supported bridge', bridge_material='Zirconia', bridge_implants=['14','16'], bridge_pontics=['15'] after the run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iteration 107 test file: /app/backend/tests/test_phase1_bridge_fields_iteration107.py. PDF text extraction uses pypdf (already in env, v6.10.2). All 4 bridge_* fields are TOP-LEVEL on the procedure document, not nested in phase1_data - the persistence test explicitly asserts that. The PDF section title 'Phase 1 - Diagnosis and Treatment Planning' is rendered with hyphen replaced by '-' due to FPDF latin-1 encoding; pypdf returns it as 'Phase 1' substring which is what the regression test checks.
rca of the issue: No production issues. One self-corrected test problem: initial PDF assertion used a naive blob.decode('latin-1') which cannot read FlateDecode-compressed page streams emitted by fpdf2; switched to pypdf.PdfReader().extract_text() and all 12 tests pass.
